notes: NOAA CPC operational bands, now fed by the RONI (Relative ONI) — Niño-3.4 anomaly minus the all-tropics mean, NOAA's monitoring standard since 1 Feb 2026. El Niño still declared at >+0.5°C for five consecutive seasons, so bands are unchanged. ≤0.5 neutral; 0.5–1.0 weak El Niño (watch); 1.0–1.5 moderate (alert); >1.5 strong/very strong (emergency-class). Symmetric La Niña handled by absolute value upstream.
NOAA CPC standardized SOI (range ~-4..+4, NOT BoM's ×10 scale). Sustained negative = El Niño. >-0.7 neutral; -0.7 to -1.0 watch; -1.0 to -1.5 alert; <-1.5 strong event. NOAA -1.0 ≈ BoM -10.

unit: m/s 850 mb zonal wind anomaly (West Pacific)
notes: NOAA CPC wpac850. Negative = weakened/westerly trades = warm water pushed east = leading El Niño trigger (precedes SST by weeks–months). >-0.5 neutral; -0.5 to -1.0 watch; -1.0 to -1.5 alert; <-1.5 strong westerly burst regime.

LIVE — NMME dynamical forecast (NOAA-GFDL SPEAR ensemble mean, via IRI CCSR open OPeNDAP). Projected Niño-3.4 SST anomaly for the forward 3-month window. Same bands as observed ONI; symmetric (El Niño high / La Niña low). Forward-looking complement to the observed ONI gauge. As a forecast it never raises the CURRENT national alert (see risk-engine NON_ALERT_KEYS).
Niño 3.4 monthly SST anomaly. Aligned with ONI; included separately so weekly SST can escalate before the 3-month ONI does.
PNG highland drought thresholds. -20% near-normal; -40% drought watch; -60% severe drought (1997 and 2015 events both >-60%). Inverted: more negative = worse.
Heat-stress proxy. Combined with rainfall deficit it drives crop and health risk in the highlands.
NASA SMAP-derived percentile. Inverted: lower percentile = drier = worse. <10th percentile = exceptional drought.
MODIS NDVI anomaly. Vegetation stress lags rainfall by ~4–8 weeks; an early canary for food-security collapse.

unit: % of cropland with Mean VHI below 35
notes: FAO GIEWS Agricultural Stress Index: share of provincial cropland where the season's Mean Vegetation Health Index fell below 35. Non-inverted, one-sided: higher = more crops under drought stress. <15% routine; 15-30% watch; 30-50% serious agricultural drought; >50% widespread crop failure. Province-level, dekadal.

unit: count of M4.5+ events in PNG (30-day window)
notes: USGS FDSNWS earthquake tempo for the PNG bbox. PNG sits on the Ring of Fire; a typical month sees ~10–25 M4.5+ events. Elevated tempo (>25) flags a seismically active period; a major shock or swarm pushes >45. Non-inverted, one-sided: higher count = worse, never negative.

unit: estimated malaria cases per 1,000 population at risk
notes: WHO GHO modelled malaria incidence (MALARIA_EST_INCIDENCE), national, annual. PNG carries one of the Western Pacific's heaviest malaria burdens — recent years run ~140–180/1,000, so that range is PNG's NORMAL, not a crisis. Bands are calibrated against that endemic baseline so a chronic heavy burden reads AMBER (watch), NOT RED: <120 low for PNG; 120–200 the standing endemic load (watch); 200–300 a genuine surge (alert); >300 epidemic-scale. Climate-sensitive (ENSO drought/flood shift breeding). Non-inverted, one-sided: higher = worse. Excluded from the national ENSO alert rollup (a structural baseline must not pin the alert); drives the Public Health sector + its own gauge.

World Bank Open Data CPI inflation (FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG), national, annual. ENSO drought reaches the economy through food and fuel prices. <5% routine; 5–10% elevated cost-of-living pressure; 10–20% high; >20% crisis inflation. Non-inverted, one-sided: higher = worse. Annual series lags 1–2 years (gauge age surfaces this).

FAOSTAT prevalence of undernourishment (item 21004), national, 3-year rolling mean. The standing food-insecurity floor an ENSO drought shock lands on. PNG runs persistently elevated, so bands are calibrated against that baseline: <10% low; 10–25% the standing floor (watch); 25–40% high; >40% acute structural hunger. Non-inverted, one-sided: higher = worse. A slow structural baseline — excluded from the national ENSO alert rollup (see risk-engine NON_ALERT_KEYS); drives the Food Security floor + its own gauge. Annual 3-yr mean lags ~2 years (gauge age surfaces this).

Open-Meteo ERA5 7-day rainfall anomaly, refreshed daily. Same inverted drought ladder as RAINFALL_ANOM: -20% near-normal; -40% drought watch; -60% severe. A fast daily complement to the CHIRPS dekadal RAINFALL_ANOM — a non-ENSO short-window signal, excluded from the national alert (see risk-engine NON_ALERT_KEYS).

Open-Meteo ERA5 7-day mean daily-max 10 m wind, as % above the 8-yr normal. One-sided (high = worse): 25–50% above normal = watch; 50–100% = alert; >100% = strong wind regime. Short-window local hazard, excluded from the national ENSO alert (see risk-engine NON_ALERT_KEYS); escalates the per-province Disaster & Hazard cell via storm-days.

CONFIG ROW, not a classified indicator. The storm-day cutoff: a day counts as a storm day when any focus-province daily-max wind reaches this speed. 10.8 m/s = Beaufort 6. Consumed by ingest/lib.ts and passed to countStormDays; edit here to retune without a code change.
